description:
Implement the Restate service types based on the specification in bead oya-frontend-btv.

## Context
Previous bead: oya-frontend-btv (specification complete)

## Success Criteria
- ServiceKind enum with 3 variants (Service, VirtualObject, Workflow)
- ContextType enum with 2 variants (Synchronous, Asynchronous)
- PortType enum with 6 variants (Any, Event, State, Signal, FlowControl, Json)
- types_compatible() function with correct logic
- check_connection() function with ContextType checking
- All 24 WorkflowNode variants have correct service_kind() and context_type()

## Scope
- Implement ServiceKind, ContextType, PortType enums
- Implement types_compatible() function
- Implement check_connection() function  
- Implement get_node_by_id() helper
- Update WorkflowNode impls for service_kind(), context_type(), input/output_port_type()
- Write unit tests for all types and functions
- Write integration tests for connection validation
- Write proptest invariants
- Write Kani model checking harnesses

## Non-goals
- Actual Restate client code (separate bead)
- Service deployment (separate bead)

## References
- .beads/oya-frontend-btv/contract.md
- .beads/oya-frontend-btv/test-plan.md
- docs/10_RESTATE_SDK.md

close_reason: Core workflow execution engine implemented. All 458 unit tests and 294 integration tests pass. Moon CI green. QA passed. DDD architecture applied with <300 line files.
description:
Implement the core workflow execution engine that runs workflows entirely in the browser via WASM.

## Context
Previous beads completed:
- bd-1c8: ExecutionState enum for node runtime status
- bd-184: Async IO Bridge  
- bd-1cu: Robustness (Cycles & Timeouts)
- bd-1k0: ServiceKind and ContextType enums (OPEN - priority 0)

## Success Criteria
- Workflows execute entirely in the browser's WASM runtime
- Node-to-node data transfer produces correct results
- Execution context is passed between nodes
- Cycles are handled without infinite loops
- Memory usage stays under 50MB for typical workflows

## Scope
- Core execution loop
- Node execution handlers
- Execution context management
- DAG traversal and scheduling
- Error isolation per node
- Timeout handling

## Non-goals
- Server-side execution
- Real-time collaboration
- Complex branching logic (deferred)

## References
- specs/flow-wasm-v1.yaml: workflow-execution behavior
- src/graph/execution.rs: existing patterns
title: engine: Implement core workflow execution engine

close_reason: Decided to keep separate ConnectionError enums with clear module boundaries: connectivity::ConnectionError for graph edit-time validation (self-connections, cycles, missing nodes), connection_errors::ConnectionError for Restate service type validation (port types, service kinds, context types). Both exported with distinct aliases (GraphConnectionError/ConnectivityConnectionError vs RestateConnectionError) and ConnectionError re-exported for backward compatibility.
description:
Black Hat Code Review found that the bead created src/graph/connection_errors.rs with a ConnectionError enum, but the existing codebase has src/graph/connectivity.rs with its own ConnectionError enum.

The two enums have different purposes:
- connectivity.rs ConnectionError: Graph connectivity validation (self-connections, cycles, missing nodes, duplicate edges, type mismatches)
- connection_errors.rs ConnectionError: Restate service type validation (port type compatibility, service kind compatibility, context type compatibility)

DECISION REQUIRED: Should these be:
1. Merged into a single unified ConnectionError enum?
2. Kept separate with clear module boundaries?
3. The new connection_errors.rs should replace connectivity.rs entirely?

This bead tracks the architectural decision and implementation of the chosen approach.
title: Architectural decision: ConnectionError enum consolidation

close_reason: Completed - Self-connection detection implemented and verified. All 40 tests pass. Critical bug fixed. Architectural follow-up bead oya-frontend-9ov created for remaining improvements.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that the workflow execution engine does NOT detect self-connections in validate_connection_validity(). Self-connections are silently accepted and can cause infinite loops in execution_queue processing, stack overflow during recursive operations, and undefined behavior in dependency resolution. The contract specifies Invariant I3: No Self-Connections but this is violated. Fix required: Add self-connection validation in validate_connection_validity() that checks if conn.source == conn.target and reports error.
title: [Red Queen] CRITICAL: Self-connection cycle detection missing - can cause infinite loops

close_reason: Fixed: Added validate_unique_node_ids() function to validate_workflow() that checks for duplicate node IDs using HashSet. Returns ValidationIssue with ERROR severity for each duplicate found.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that the workflow execution engine does NOT validate unique node IDs. Duplicate node IDs are silently accepted and cause undefined behavior during execution_queue processing. The contract specifies Invariant I1: Unique Node IDs but this is violated. Fix required: Add duplicate node ID validation in validate_workflow() that checks for duplicate IDs in workflow.nodes.
title: [Red Queen] CRITICAL: Duplicate node ID validation missing - causes undefined behavior

close_reason: Completed: Expression depth limiting feature implemented with all tests passing
description: Adversarial testing revealed that resolve_expressions() has no depth limit for nested expressions. Deeply nested expressions (e.g., level1000) can cause stack overflow. Fix required: Add depth limit parameter to resolve_expressions() and return error when limit exceeded.
title: [Red Queen] MAJOR: Expression depth limit missing - stack overflow risk

close_reason: Completed all quality gates: clippy passes, 1340 tests pass, all defects fixed, architectural drift check passed
description:
The test suite for bead oya-frontend-1af passed all critical gates but has known gaps:
1. 122 instances of 'let _ =' error suppression that should be handled properly
2. 31 tests using 'fn test_*' prefix instead of BDD naming
3. Test density 1.30x vs 5x target
4. Coverage 62.43% vs 90% target
5. 5 modules at 0% coverage: validation, service_calls, node_ui_state, validation_checks/*

This bead tracks the work to improve test hygiene and coverage before final bead closure.
title: Test suite improvements - coverage and hygiene

description:
Address architectural issues found by black hat review for bead oya-frontend-5uz:

1. Split add_connection_checked (44 lines) into ≤25-line functions
2. Replace boolean return in add_connection with Result type
3. Add newtype for PortType in ConnectionError::TypeMismatch
4. Replace Option<PortType> with Result<PortType, PortTypeParseError>
5. Separate validation logic from state mutation (Functional Core/Shell)
6. Remove public helper functions (path_exists_internal, check_port_type_compatibility_internal)
7. Add doc tests for contract examples

This bead should be addressed after oya-frontend-5uz is closed to ensure the critical bug fix is released first.
title: ARCH: Refactor connectivity module for architectural compliance - Fix black hat findings

close_reason: Specification phase complete. Contract and test-plan created for Restate service types integration. Ready for implementation phase.
description:
Integrate Restate service types (ServiceKind and ContextType) into the workflow execution engine.

## Context
Previous beads completed:
- oya-frontend-2mn: Core workflow execution engine
- bd-1c8: ExecutionState enum for node runtime status
- bd-184: Async IO Bridge
- bd-1cu: Robustness (Cycles & Timeouts)

## Success Criteria
- ServiceKind enum properly aligned with Restate SDK
- ContextType enum for workflow vs service contexts
- Node type inference from ServiceKind
- Type-safe connection validation based on service kinds
- All 24 node types properly categorized

## Scope
- Add ServiceKind enum (Service, VirtualObject, Workflow)
- Add ContextType enum (ServiceContext, WorkflowContext)
- Implement type inference from WorkflowNode
- Update connection validation to check ServiceKind compatibility
- Add Restate-specific node validation

## Non-goals
- Actual Restate client implementation (separate bead)
- Service deployment configuration (separate bead)

## References
- docs/10_RESTATE_SDK.md
- specs/flow-wasm-v1.yaml
- src/graph/restate_types.rs
title: restate: Integrate Restate service types and context handling

close_reason: Fixed: Implemented ExecutionConfig struct with timeout_ms, memory_limit_bytes, max_iterations, continue_on_error, skip_failed_nodes, and max_expression_depth fields. All unit tests passing.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that the ExecutionConfig with timeout_ms from the contract is NOT IMPLEMENTED. The execute_workflow function with timeout enforcement does not exist. Nodes can run indefinitely without any timeout. The contract specifies ExecutionConfig.timeout_ms and ExecutionError::Timeout variant but neither is implemented. Fix required: Implement ExecutionConfig, execute_workflow with timeout enforcement, and ExecutionError enum with Timeout variant.
title: [Red Queen] CRITICAL: Timeout enforcement not implemented - contract violation
[Red Queen] CRITICAL: quality-gate-no-panic: cargo clippy -- -D clippy::unwrap_used -D clippy::expect_used -D clippy::panic
[Red Queen] CRITICAL: contract: cargo test --lib -- connectivity::PortType::parse --nocapture

close_reason: Cycle detection fix implemented. All tests pass. Silent exclusion bug resolved.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that when cycles are detected, the prepare_run() function using Kahn's algorithm silently excludes nodes in cycles from the execution_queue. No error is reported, no warning is generated. The workflow appears to succeed but with missing nodes. Fix required: Either implement proper cycle detection that reports cycles, or implement iterative execution with maximum iteration count and timeout.
title: [Red Queen] MAJOR: Cycle handling is non-transparent - silently excludes nodes

description:
Fix critical architectural issues in connectivity module identified by black hat review:

1. ConnectionId uses wrong type (uuid::Uuid instead of u64) and has public field
2. PortType uses named field struct instead of tuple newtype
3. Duplicate ConnectionError enum in validation.rs
4. validate_connection missing path parameters per contract
5. list_connections returns Vec instead of Result<Vec>

This bead fixes all issues identified in oya-frontend-9ov that persisted through multiple repair loops.
title: ARCH: Fix connectivity module newtype and signature issues

close_reason: Memory limit enforcement implemented. Added: 1) Memory tracking field to Workflow struct, 2) estimate_memory_usage() function for conservative JSON size estimation, 3) check_and_update_memory() enforcement in execution flow, 4) MemoryLimitExceeded error propagation. All 210 tests pass including 12 new memory-specific tests.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that there is no memory limit enforcement in the workflow execution engine. Large outputs (e.g., 100MB JSON) can be created without any limits. Fix required: Implement ExecutionConfig.memory_limit_bytes and enforce during node execution.
title: [Red Queen] MAJOR: Memory limit enforcement missing - arbitrary memory usage possible

close_reason: Completed - Execution state machine fully implemented with comprehensive test coverage, fuzz targets, and QA Enforcer verification. All 100 tests pass. Code pushed to remote.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that ExecutionState can be directly mutated without any validation. Transitions like Completed -> Running are possible via direct field assignment. The contract specifies Invariant I9: State Machine Compliance but there is no enforcement. The try_transition() function exists but is not used to enforce transitions. Fix required: Either use type-safe state machine (sealed trait pattern) or enforce transitions via try_transition() at all mutation points.
title: [Red Queen] MAJOR: State machine enforcement missing - direct mutation bypasses rules

close_reason: Test plan created and approved. Bead oya-frontend-btv test plan (test-plan.md) is now comprehensive with 242 behaviors, 100% error variant coverage, 5 fuzz targets, and 11 mutation checkpoints. Review status: APPROVED.
description:
Bead oya-frontend-btv ('restate: Integrate Restate service types and context handling') was submitted for final review but has NO test-plan.md file. 

The Test Reviewer skill requires both contract.md and test-plan.md for Mode 1 Plan Inquisition, or implementation + existing tests for Mode 2 Suite Inquisition.

Current state:
- Implementation exists in src/graph/service_kinds.rs
- Tests exist in tests/flow_extender_contracts.rs (but these are for flow_extender module, NOT for the restate_types module)
- NO test-plan.md exists for the bead

The user's fix notes mention:
1. Added ContextTypeMismatch error handling to check_connection()
2. Added get_node_by_id() function that uses NodeNotFound
3. Fixed Node → WorkflowNode in check_connection() signature
4. Fixed wildcard assertions in test plan

BUT: No test-plan.md file exists to verify these claims. No implementation of check_connection() or get_node_by_id() exists in the codebase.

MANDATE: A test-plan.md must be created for bead oya-frontend-btv before it can be approved. The test plan must cover:
- ServiceKind enum parsing (Handler, Workflow, Actor variants)
- ContextType enum parsing (Synchronous, Asynchronous variants, with sync/async aliases)
- ParseServiceKindError error variant with exact error assertions
- ParseContextTypeError error variant with exact error assertions
- ConnectionError::TypeMismatch variant (already has tests in connectivity.rs but need to verify it covers ServiceKind-based validation)
- get_node_by_id() function if it exists
- check_connection() function if it exists

Without a test-plan.md, the bead cannot pass the Plan Inquisition gate.
title: Test plan required for bead oya-frontend-btv - Restate types and context handling

close_reason: Fixed: Implemented WorkflowExecutionError enum and ExecutionConfig struct with all contract-specified variants. All unit tests passing.
description: Adversarial testing revealed that ExecutionError enum from contract does NOT exist in current code. Variants like CycleDetected, NodeNotFound, ExecutionFailed, Timeout, MemoryLimitExceeded, InvalidConfig, NoEntryNodes, EmptyWorkflow, InvalidStateTransition are all missing. This is a fundamental contract violation. Fix required: Implement ExecutionError enum with all contract-specified variants.
